_provenance.stage2: engine/output/isa-samad-federal-court-rule-137-review-stage2.json (Gemini, bias_score 0, one MISLEADING_FRAMING flag on 5-vs-10 Feb timeline)
_provenance.stage4: engine/output/isa-samad-federal-court-rule-137-review-stage4.json (DeepSeek alt-framing, completeness 42, settler/Sarawak scope-expansion declined; precision facts incorporated into discipline)
_provenance.stage5: engine/output/isa-samad-federal-court-rule-137-review-stage5.json (Grok contrarian, courage 41, elite-impunity political framing declined per CLAUDE.md process-not-people discipline)
revision_history: v1 (2026-05-17): 6-stage synthesis. Stage 2 MISLEADING_FRAMING fix (the prosecution's invitation claim was about the 5 February hearing, not 10 February) applied to hook sub and Card 3 big. Stage 3 corrections applied: (a) 'convict again 2026' → 'restore 2026'; (b) 'Felda's RM160 million purchase ... in 2015' → 'a Felda subsidiary's 2014 approval of the RM160 million Merdeka Palace Hotel purchase'; (c) MACC Act sections list cleaned to s.16(a)(A) + s.24(1) + s.50(1), dropping unverified s.17(a); (d) 'Both stand on the record' → 'Both positions were stated in open court'; (e) Rule 137 standard rewritten to drop 'only' and include 'breach of natural justice' as a recognised ground; (f) reframe rewritten from 'why the routine record is not the record' (overclaim — court did not find the official record failed) to 'why courtroom audio is not part of the routine record' (factually anchored to CRT discretion); (g) analogy sub dropped 'the next bench' (no public source for that expansion); (h) view card kicker tightened from 'whether the third round was heard' to 'whether the defence was given the floor' (more precise about what the dispute is about per Stage 3, and preserves the kicker structure Stage 5 endorsed as the strongest card).
_provenance.discipline: Process-not-people; live-application restraint; 3R-clean. The Rule 137 application is pending; case management on 14 May 2026 was scheduled to fix the next substantive hearing date and that date is not in the public reporting consulted. Cards do NOT adjudicate which on-record account is correct; the CRT is the neutral arbiter the court itself ordered for that purpose. Cards do NOT characterise any sitting judge personally. Cards do NOT re-litigate Isa Samad's guilt — three courts have ruled on that and the live application is procedural. Stage 5 pressed for an 'elite access to endless process' political reframe; declined because Rule 137 is a procedural right available in principle to any convicted person, and the editorial line at T4A is anger-at-process, not anger-at-class. Stage 4 pressed for a Felda-settlers / Sarawak / bailout scope expansion; declined because it would dilute the apex-court-record frame this issue exists to surface, though those facts remain verified and on the record for future related issues.

headline: Apex court releases courtroom audio after a dispute over what its bench said.
context: On 13 May 2026, the Federal Court adjourned former Felda chairman Isa Samad's conviction review. The defence and the prosecution gave conflicting accounts of what happened on 5 and 10 February. The bench ordered the courtroom audio released to both sides.

cards.big: On 13 May, the apex court adjourned Isa Samad's review and ordered the courtroom audio released to both sides.
cards.sub: The defence says the bench never invited it to submit before its 10 February verdict. The prosecution says it did on 5 February, and the defence declined.
revision_tags: REPHRASED (Stage 2 bias): old sub 'The defence says the 10 February bench never invited it to submit on the merits. The prosecution says it did, and the defence declined.' → new sub anchors the DPP's invitation claim correctly on the 5 February hearing (not the 10 February bench). This is precisely why the court ordered CRT for BOTH 5 February and 10 February.
sources: The Star, 13 May 2026, 'Federal Court defers Isa Samad review bid, allows access to court transcripts': https://www.thestar.com.my/news/nation/2026/05/13/federal-court-defers-isa-samad-review-bid-allows-access-to-court-transcripts

Malay Mail, 13 May 2026, 'Federal Court adjourns Isa Samad's bid to review reinstated corruption conviction': https://www.malaymail.com/news/malaysia/2026/05/13/federal-court-adjourns-isa-samads-bid-to-review-reinstated-corruption-conviction/219771

Free Malaysia Today, 13 May 2026, 'Federal Court adjourns Isa Samad's bid to overturn conviction': https://www.freemalaysiatoday.com/category/nation/2026/05/13/federal-court-adjourns-isa-samads-bid-to-overturn-conviction

NST, 13 May 2026, 'Federal Court adjourns Isa Samad's review bid over corruption sentence': https://www.nst.com.my/news/nation/2026/05/1438521/federal-court-adjourns-isa-samads-review-bid-over-corruption-sentence
The Edge, 13 May 2026, 'Isa's conviction review adjourned over court recordings dispute': https://theedgemalaysia.com/node/803361

cards.big: Three courts heard the same nine corruption charges and reached three different conclusions: convict 2021, acquit 2024, restore 2026.
cards.sub: RM3.09 million in alleged gratification, tied to a Felda subsidiary's 2014 approval of the RM160 million Merdeka Palace Hotel purchase in Kuching.
revision_tags: CORRECTED (Stage 3): big 'convict again 2026' → 'restore 2026'. The Federal Court restored the High Court conviction rather than conducting a fresh trial.

CORRECTED (Stage 3): sub 'Felda's RM160 million purchase of Merdeka Palace Hotel in Kuching in 2015' → 'a Felda subsidiary's 2014 approval of the RM160 million Merdeka Palace Hotel purchase in Kuching'. The purchaser was FICSB; board approval and acceptance occurred in 2014, with alleged payments spanning 2014-2015.
CORRECTED (Stage 3): sub 'kickbacks' → 'gratification'. 'Gratification' is the MACC Act statutory term and the framing used in the judgment text.
sources: Malay Mail, 3 February 2021, 'Former Felda chief Isa Samad convicted of nine corruption charges': https://www.malaymail.com/news/malaysia/2021/02/03/umnos-isa-samad-convicted-of-nine-corruption-charges/1946479
The Star, 6 March 2024, 'Court of Appeal acquits Mohd Isa Samad of corruption charges': https://www.thestar.com.my/news/nation/2024/03/06/court-of-appeal-acquits-mohd-isa-samad-of-corruption-charges
Court of Appeal Broad Grounds of Judgment, Mohd Isa Abdul Samad v Public Prosecutor, 6 March 2024: https://www.kehakiman.gov.my/sites/default/files/2024-03/W-05%28SH%29-28-02-2021.pdf
Federal Court judgment text, Public Prosecutor v Mohd Isa Abdul Samad, 10 February 2026: https://www.elaw.my/JE/01/JE_2026_12.html
Bernama, 10 February 2026, 'Federal Court Reinstates Isa Samad's Conviction And Sentence, Issues Warrant Of Committal': https://www.bernama.com/en/news.php?id=2522288

NST, 10 February 2026, 'Why the Federal Court reinstated Isa Samad's corruption conviction': https://www.nst.com.my/news/nation/2026/02/1375192/why-federal-court-reinstated-isa-samads-corruption-conviction

cards.big: M Athimulan says he was never invited to submit before the verdict. DPP Afzainizam Abdul Aziz says the 5 February bench did invite, and Athimulan declined.
cards.sub: Both positions were stated in open court. The Chief Judge of Malaya ordered the audio of 5 and 10 February released to both sides on 13 May.
revision_tags: REPHRASED (Stage 2 bias): big rewritten to anchor the DPP's invitation claim correctly on the 5 February hearing (matches the original Bernama/Star reporting and the brief discipline of attributing both positions accurately).

CORRECTED (Stage 3): sub 'Both stand on the record' → 'Both positions were stated in open court'. The original phrasing risked implying both positions are already established in the formal court record or CRT, which is not publicly verified.
sources: The Star, 13 May 2026, 'Federal Court defers Isa Samad review bid, allows access to court transcripts': https://www.thestar.com.my/news/nation/2026/05/13/federal-court-defers-isa-samad-review-bid-allows-access-to-court-transcripts

Malay Mail, 13 May 2026, 'Federal Court adjourns Isa Samad's bid to review reinstated corruption conviction': https://www.malaymail.com/news/malaysia/2026/05/13/federal-court-adjourns-isa-samads-bid-to-review-reinstated-corruption-conviction/219771

Free Malaysia Today, 13 May 2026, 'Federal Court adjourns Isa Samad's bid to overturn conviction': https://www.freemalaysiatoday.com/category/nation/2026/05/13/federal-court-adjourns-isa-samads-bid-to-overturn-conviction

NST, 13 May 2026, 'Federal Court adjourns Isa Samad's review bid over corruption sentence': https://www.nst.com.my/news/nation/2026/05/1438521/federal-court-adjourns-isa-samads-review-bid-over-corruption-sentence
The Edge, 13 May 2026, 'Isa's conviction review adjourned over court recordings dispute': https://theedgemalaysia.com/node/803361

The Star, 7 March 2026, 'Isa Samad files review against Federal Court's decision': https://www.thestar.com.my/news/nation/2026/03/07/isa-samad-files-review-against-federal-court039s-decision-restoring-corruption-conviction-fine-and-jail-term

cards.big: Rule 137 reviews of an apex ruling are granted in rare situations: quorum failure, bias, breach of natural justice, or integrity critically undermined.
cards.sub: Najib Razak's Rule 137 bid to revisit his SRC conviction was dismissed 4-1 on 31 March 2023. The dissenting judge cited breach of natural justice.
revision_tags: CORRECTED (Stage 3): big rewritten. Old wording 'granted only in rare circumstances: lack of quorum, established bias, or integrity critically undermined' overstated with the word 'only' and omitted the recognised natural-justice / fair-hearing ground, which is directly relevant to Isa Samad's review claim. New wording drops 'only' and lists 'breach of natural justice' explicitly alongside the other categories.
sources: Rules of the Federal Court 1995, Rule 137 (P.U.(A) 376/1995): https://tcclaw.com.my/wp-content/uploads/2025/08/RULES-OF-THE-FEDERAL-COURT-1995.pdf
Cecil Abraham & Partners / Lexology, 'The Invocation of the Review Jurisdiction of the Federal Court of Malaysia': https://www.lexology.com/library/detail.aspx?g=9edb0e1b-ea8a-4e8e-851d-4d003cbbf917
Joshua Wu Kai-Ming, '5 Things About The Review of Federal Court Decisions': https://joshuawu.my/5-things-about-the-review-of-federal-court-decisions/
Federal Court judgment, Najib Razak SRC Rule 137 review, March 2023: https://www.elaw.my/JE/01/JE_2023_12.html

Malaysian Bar press release, 7 April 2023, on Najib Rule 137 dismissal: https://www.malaysianbar.org.my/article/news/legal-and-general-news/legal-news/najib-fails-to-get-second-review-of-src-conviction-and-sentence-federal-court-rules
The Star, 31 March 2023, 'Najib loses bid to review Federal Court decision': https://www.thestar.com.my/news/nation/2023/03/31/najib-loses-bid-to-review-federal-court-decision

Malay Mail, 30 March 2023, 'No more way out for Najib as apex court refuses SRC review': https://www.malaymail.com/news/malaysia/2023/03/30/no-more-way-out-for-najib-as-apex-court-refuses-src-review/62356

cards.big: When two officers of the court give irreconcilable accounts of what the apex court said in open session, the question is not who is right. It is why courtroom audio is not part of the routine record.
revision_tags: CORRECTED (Stage 3): 'It is why the routine record is not the record.' → 'It is why courtroom audio is not part of the routine record.' Stage 3 flagged the original as OVERCLAIM because the court did not find the official record failed; it allowed CRT access to resolve a dispute. The new wording is factually anchored to the verified Visvanathan (Malay Mail, 2020) and Shafee Abdullah (Edge, December 2024) observations that CRT access in Malaysia is discretionary rather than routine, while preserving the reframe's question-restructuring shape that Stage 5 endorsed as the strongest card.
sources: Editorial synthesis grounded in: (a) the 13 May 2026 Federal Court order releasing the CRT to both sides because the paper minute could not by itself resolve the dispute (sources cited on Card 3); (b) Federal Constitution Article 5(1) and the audi alteram partem doctrine; (c) Rule 137 jurisprudence on 'rare and exceptional' circumstances including critical undermining of decision integrity (Cecil Abraham & Partners memo cited on Card 4); (d) M. Visvanathan, Malay Mail commentary, 24 April 2020, noting CRT recordings 'have not always been given to litigants' lawyers' (Stage 4 verification): https://www.malaymail.com/news/what-you-think/2020/04/24/have-we-weighed-the-risks-of-live-streaming-court-proceedings-m.-visvanatha/1859741; (e) Shafee Abdullah, December 2024, on new procedure requiring written application and three-day wait for CRT access (Stage 4 verification): https://theedgemalaysia.com/node/767984.

cards.big: Like a referee's match report contested by both teams, where the only neutral arbiter left is the video.
cards.sub: The Federal Court has now ordered the audio of two earlier hearings released to both sides to test what the previous bench said.
revision_tags: CORRECTED (Stage 3): sub 'released so both sides, and the next bench, can hear what the previous bench said' → 'released to both sides to test what the previous bench said'. Stage 3 found no public source for the court expressly ordering CRT access for the next bench's use; the order was for the parties.
sources: Editorial analogy. Anchored to the 13 May 2026 Federal Court order releasing the CRT for 5 February and 10 February to both parties (sources cited on the hook and Card 3).

cards.big: Three courts heard the same nine charges and reached three different conclusions. The fourth round is not about whether Isa is guilty. It is about whether the defence was given the floor.
revision_tags: CORRECTED (Stage 3) + STRENGTHENED (Stage 5 weakest-card): kicker 'whether the third round was heard' → 'whether the defence was given the floor'. Stage 3 cautioned that 'whether the third round was heard' could imply the entire Federal Court appeal was unheard, when the dispute is specifically about whether the defence was invited to submit on merits and mitigation. The new wording is more precise on what the dispute is about, retains the kicker structure that Stage 5 endorsed (card 5 strongest), and passes the WhatsApp test as a stand-alone sentence.
sources: Editorial synthesis. Verified facts: High Court conviction 3 February 2021 (Malay Mail, The Vibes); Court of Appeal acquittal 6 March 2024 (The Star, Malay Mail, BenarNews, Court of Appeal Broad Grounds); Federal Court reinstatement 10 February 2026 (Bernama, NST, FCC Times, Federal Court judgment text); Rule 137 review live, adjourned 13 May 2026 (The Star, Malay Mail, FMT, NST, Edge — all 13 May).

Statutory and procedural primary text: Rules of the Federal Court 1995 Rule 137; MACC Act 2009 sections 16(a)(A), 24(1), and 50(1); Federal Constitution Articles 5(1), 8(1), 145. Court documents: Court of Appeal Broad Grounds of Judgment (6 March 2024) and Federal Court judgment text (10 February 2026). News and reporting: Bernama, NST, The Star, Malay Mail, Free Malaysia Today, The Edge, Malaysiakini, Sinar Daily, The Vibes, BenarNews, FCC Times, MalaysiaNow. Practitioner / academic notes on Rule 137 jurisprudence: Cecil Abraham & Partners via Lexology; Joshua Wu Kai-Ming; Neoh Kai Sheng. CRT-access observations: M. Visvanathan, Malay Mail (2020); Shafee Abdullah, The Edge (December 2024); Malaysian Bar (April 2023 statement on Najib SRC review).
confidence: High on the structural facts of all three rulings (dates, panel compositions where named, sentencing structure, MACC Act s.50(1) presumption invocation); High on the 13 May 2026 review adjournment (panel chaired by Chief Judge of Malaya Hashim Hamzah, CRT release ordered for 5 and 10 February, both DPP and defence positions on record); High on the Najib SRC Rule 137 4-1 dismissal denominator (31 March 2023, dissent on natural-justice grounds); High on the MACC Act s.16(a)(A) + s.24(1) sentencing formula (5x gratification = RM15.45M from RM3.09M) per Stage 3 verification. Medium on the 'rare and exceptional' Rule 137 standard's exact contours: drawn from practitioner notes consistent with the Rule text but not from a controlling case citation; the wording chosen does not overstate. Medium on the next substantive hearing date — case management was scheduled for 14 May 2026; no later public report consulted gives the substantive hearing date.

introduced_claims.claim: Anchoring the DPP's invitation claim on the 5 February hearing rather than the 10 February bench (Card 1 sub, Card 3 big).
introduced_claims.primary_source: Stage 2 (Gemini, 17 May 2026): MISLEADING_FRAMING flag with fix wording explicitly referencing 5 February as the prosecution's stated invitation date. Cross-verified against The Star, 13 May 2026 (cited Stage 1 source) and Bernama, 13 May 2026.
introduced_claims.verification_note: This is not a new factual claim; it corrects the original analysis's compressed phrasing to match what the public reporting actually attributes to DPP Afzainizam Abdul Aziz.

introduced_claims.primary_source: MACC Act 2009 s.16(a)(A) and s.24(1) statutory text, plus Federal Court judgment text (10 February 2026, Stage 3 source).
introduced_claims.verification_note: Statutory term, more precise; does not change the substance of the alleged offence.

introduced_claims.claim: Re-phrasing the reframe's second clause to 'why courtroom audio is not part of the routine record' grounded on the Visvanathan / Shafee CRT-discretion observations.
introduced_claims.primary_source: M. Visvanathan, Malay Mail, 24 April 2020 (Stage 4 verification); Shafee Abdullah, The Edge, December 2024 (Stage 4 verification).
introduced_claims.verification_note: The original phrasing 'why the routine record is not the record' was flagged OVERCLAIM by Stage 3 because the court did not find the official record failed. The new phrasing is factually anchored to the verified observation that CRT access in Malaysia is discretionary and requires application, rather than being part of the routine record by default.

synthesis_notes: Three over-integration decisions, each explained: (1) STAGE 5 vs CLAUDE.md discipline. Stage 5 (courage 41) pressed an 'elite access to endless process' political reframe and called the original 'process-not-people' framing 'gutless legal journalism.' Stage 2 (bias 0) found the symmetric treatment of court officers to be impartial, and Stage 3 verified the original procedural framing as on-record. Chose Stages 2 + 3 over Stage 5 because (a) Rule 137 is a procedural right available in principle to any convicted person, so 'elite access' is not what distinguishes this case from any other Rule 137 application; (b) Stage 5's preferred frame is anger-at-class which violates CLAUDE.md's anger-at-process editorial line; (c) Stage 5's claim that the symmetry is 'false balance' is itself wrong on the facts — the CRT release order is precisely the mechanism that will resolve the symmetry, and the synthesis preserves both on-record positions until the CRT is in evidence. (2) STAGE 4 vs frame discipline. Stage 4 (completeness 42) wanted Felda settlers, Sarawak, the RM110M-vs-RM160M overpayment, the RM8.3B bailout, and international parallels (India curative petition, Singapore s.394H, UK CCRC) brought into the cards. Each of those facts is verified and adds context, but adding any of them dilutes the apex-court-record frame this issue exists to surface. Declined for the cards; preserved as verified context in discipline + sources fields for future related issues to draw on. (3) STAGE 3 weakening vs Stage 5 sharpening on the view card. Stage 3 flagged 'whether the third round was heard' as MISLEADING (could imply the entire Federal Court appeal was unheard); Stage 5 flagged the same card as the weakest (too procedural). Chose Stage 3's precision concern as primary — the kicker must accurately describe what the dispute is about — and used the rewrite ('whether the defence was given the floor') to also retain the kicker structure Stage 5 endorsed on card 5. Did not average toward Stage 5's preferred 'elite access' framing.
needs_verification_remaining: Whether the written grounds of the 10 February 2026 Federal Court ruling have been publicly hosted in a form that resolves the natural-justice / submission-invitation question directly. The brief stage1 noted this as needs_verification; Stage 4 cited the elaw.my host. Stage 3 did not directly retrieve and quote the passage. Phase 6 fact-check should attempt to retrieve.

The new substantive hearing date for the Rule 137 review, fixed at the 14 May 2026 case management. Stage 3 confirmed it was not in public reporting up to 17 May 2026. Time-sensitive — should be re-checked at Phase 6 legal-and-accuracy check.

The MACC Act statutory sentencing formula (5x gratification = fine, per s.24(1)) is consistent with the RM15.45M / RM3.09M arithmetic; Stage 3 verified the formula. The default imprisonment in lieu of fine is reported variably (two years vs cumulative 18 years across counts). Cards do not rely on the default term.

scoring.sr_justification: Integrates all five stages with discipline. All Stage 3 cardinal-sin flags actioned. Stage 2 MISLEADING_FRAMING fix applied correctly. Stage 4 over-scoped expansion declined with reasoned synthesis_notes. Stage 5 political-framing pressure declined with explicit CLAUDE.md grounding. All wording changes tagged with the correct revision tag. No INTRODUCED claim lacks primary-source backing. Two information gaps remain (10 Feb written grounds, new substantive hearing date) — both flagged in needs_verification_remaining for Phase 6 legal-and-accuracy check; neither blocks cards as currently worded since cards do not rely on them as factual assertions.
